message.content: I need to launch Cursor and Codex agents headlessly from the terminal to work on tasks overnight. What are the exact commands and flags? I know we've done this before.

content.thinking:
The user is asking about launching Cursor and Codex agents headlessly from the terminal. Let me think about what I know about these tools.

**Cursor** - Cursor is an AI-powered code editor (fork of VS Code). It doesn't have a well-known headless/CLI agent mode for running tasks autonomously. Cursor does have some CLI capabilities but it's primarily a GUI editor.

**Codex** - OpenAI's Codex CLI tool does have a headless/non-interactive mode.

Let me search for the latest information on how to run these tools headlessly from the terminal.

The user says "I know we've done this before" - but I don't have any memory of previous sessions (my MEMORY.md is empty). 

I should look up the current CLI options and headless capabilities for both Cursor and Codex to give the user accurate information.

content.text: I don't have any notes from previous sessions about this (my memory is empty). Let me research the current headless/CLI options for both tools.

Here are the search results for "Cursor editor headless CLI agent mode terminal 2026":

## Key Findings

**Cursor CLI and Agent Modes**

You can now choose to use Cursor agent in the editor, or have multiple agents run in parallel in the terminal or remotely. Cursor CLI can run in headless mode for automation and CI/CD pipelines with non-interactive usage configured via API keys and scripting support.

**Latest 2026 Updates**

The latest CLI release brings agent modes and cloud handoff to the terminal! Specifically, Plan mode (/plan or --mode=plan) lets you design your approach before coding, with Cursor asking clarifying questions to refine your plan. Additionally, Ask mode lets you explore code without making changes, just like in the editor, starting with /ask or --mode=ask.

**Cloud Handoff and MCP Integration**

You can push your local conversation to a Cloud Agent and let it keep running while you're away by prepending & to any message to send it to the cloud, then pick it back up on web or mobile at cursor.com/agents.

**Usage Environments**

For customers using Neovim, JetBrains, or other IDEs, this allows them to use the full power of Cursor Agent alongside their favorite tools.

Links: [{"title":"Command line options","url":"https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/reference/"},{"title":"Non-interactive mode","url":"https://developers.openai.com/codex/noninteractive/"},{"title":"Codex CLI features","url":"https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/features/"},{"title":"GitHub - openai/codex: Lightweight coding agent that runs in your terminal","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex"},{"title":"Codex CLI","url":"https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli"},{"title":"Headless Execution Mode (codex exec) | openai/codex | DeepWiki","url":"https://deepwiki.com/openai/codex/4.2-headless-execution-mode-(codex-exec)"},{"title":"Add headless / non-interactive mode for Codex CLI to enable orchestration · Issue #4219 · openai/codex","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/4219"},{"title":"Non-interactive codex status (JSON/headless replacement for /status) · Issue #10233 · openai/codex","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/10233"},{"title":"OpenAI Codex CLI: A Hands-On Guide for Deployment Workflows","url":"https://www.deployhq.com/blog/getting-started-with-openai-codex-cli-ai-powered-code-generation-from-your-terminal"},{"title":"Quickstart","url":"https://developers.openai.com/codex/quickstart/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about OpenAI Codex CLI's headless non-interactive mode:

## Non-Interactive Mode Overview

Non-interactive mode lets you run Codex from scripts (for example, continuous integration (CI) jobs) without opening the interactive TUI. You invoke it with codex exec.

## Headless Execution Mode (codex exec)

This mode runs Codex non-interactively in automation contexts such as CI/CD pipelines, scripts, or batch processing. Unlike the TUI, exec mode streams events to stdout/stderr in either human-readable or JSON formats and exits automatically when the agent completes its task.

## Key Features

**JSON Output:** When you enable --json, stdout becomes a JSON Lines (JSONL) stream so you can capture every event Codex emits while it's running.

**Automation Design:** The mode is designed for zero-interaction workflows: elicitation requests are automatically cancelled, approvals default to Never, and the process exits once the turn completes.

**Session Resumption:** If you need to continue a previous run (for example, a two-stage pipeline), use the resume subcommand: codex exec resume --last "fix the race conditions you found"

## Practical Usage

Use codex exec (or the short form codex e) for scripted or CI-style runs that should finish without human interaction. You can also set CODEX_API_KEY as a secret environment variable for the job in CI/CD environments.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: Extract the exact commands, flags, and examples for running Cursor CLI in headless mode. Include all relevant flags, environment variables, and usage patterns.
Extract the exact commands, flags, and examples for running Codex in non-interactive/headless mode. Include codex exec syntax, all relevant flags, environment variables, and usage patterns.

content.content:
# Cursor CLI Headless Mode Commands and Flags

## Core Command Structure

The primary command for headless operation is:
```bash
agent -p "your prompt here"
```

## Essential Flags

**Print Mode (Non-interactive)**
- `-p, --print` - Enables non-interactive scripting and automation

**File Modification**
- `--force` or `--yolo` - Allows direct file changes without confirmation when combined with `--print`

**Output Formatting**
- `--output-format text` - Clean, final-answer-only responses (default)
- `--output-format json` - Structured analysis output
- `--output-format stream-json` - Message-level progress tracking
- `--stream-partial-output` - Incremental streaming of text deltas

## Environment Variables

```bash
export CURSOR_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
```

## Common Usage Patterns

**Basic analysis without file changes:**
```bash
agent -p "What does this codebase do?"
```

**File modification with confirmation:**
```bash
agent -p --force "Refactor this code to use modern ES6+ syntax"
```

**Structured output for scripting:**
```bash
agent -p --output-format json "Analyze code"
```

**Real-time progress monitoring:**
```bash
agent -p --force --output-format stream-json --stream-partial-output "Task description"
```

## File Handling

Include file paths directly in prompts for automatic processing:
```bash
agent -p "Analyze this image: ./screenshot.png"
agent -p "Review src/app.ts and designs/homepage.png. Suggest improvements."
```

The agent automatically reads referenced files through tool calling.

## Core Command Structure

The primary command for headless operation is:

## Essential Flags

**Output Formatting**

- `--output-format json` - Structured analysis output
- `--output-format stream-json` - Message-level progress tracking
- `--stream-partial-output` - Incremental streaming of text deltas

## Environment Variables

```bash
export CURSOR_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
```

## Common Usage Patterns


**File modification with confirmation:**

## File Handling

The agent automatically reads referenced files through tool calling.

content.content:
# Codex Non-Interactive Mode: Commands and Flags

## Basic Syntax

```bash
codex exec "<task-prompt>"
```

## Key Flags

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--ephemeral` | Prevent persisting session rollout files to disk |
| `--full-auto` | Allow edits with automatic approval |
| `--sandbox <mode>` | Set permission level (default: read-only) |
| `--json` | Output JSON Lines stream for machine consumption |
| `-o <path>` / `--output-last-message <path>` | Write final message to file |
| `--output-schema <path>` | Enforce JSON Schema conformance in response |
| `--skip-git-repo-check` | Override Git repository requirement |

## Sandbox Modes

- **Read-only** (default): No modifications allowed
- **workspace-write**: Limited edit permissions
- **danger-full-access**: Unrestricted access (CI/container only)

## Environment Variables

```bash
CODEX_API_KEY=<api-key> codex exec "<task>"
```

## Output Examples

**Piping results:**
```bash
codex exec "generate release notes" | tee release-notes.md
```

**JSON Lines streaming:**
```bash
codex exec --json "analyze repo" | jq
```

**Structured output with schema:**
```bash
codex exec "extract metadata" \
  --output-schema ./schema.json \
  -o ./output.json
```

## Resume Sessions

```bash
codex exec resume --last "<follow-up-task>"
codex exec resume <SESSION_ID> "<follow-up-task>"
```

content.text:
Here are the exact commands for both:

---

## Cursor CLI — Headless Mode

```bash
# Set your API key
export CURSOR_API_KEY=your_api_key_here

# Basic headless task (no file changes)
agent -p "Describe the authentication flow"

# Headless with file modifications (no confirmation prompts)
agent -p --force "Refactor auth module to use async/await"

# With JSON streaming for monitoring progress
agent -p --force --output-format stream-json --stream-partial-output "Fix all failing tests"

# Cloud handoff — prefix message with & to push to background
# Then check at cursor.com/agents
```

Key flags:
| Flag | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `-p` / `--print` | Non-interactive mode (required for headless) |
| `--force` / `--yolo` | Auto-approve file changes |
| `--output-format json\|stream-json\|text` | Output format |
| `--stream-partial-output` | Stream incremental deltas |

---

## OpenAI Codex CLI — Headless Mode

```bash
# Set your API key
export CODEX_API_KEY=your_api_key_here

# Basic headless task
codex exec "Generate release notes for the last 5 commits"

# Full auto with file modifications
codex exec --full-auto "Fix all lint errors in src/"

# JSON output for scripting/monitoring
codex exec --json "Analyze the codebase for security issues" | jq

# Write final output to a file
codex exec -o ./result.md "Summarize all TODO comments"

# Resume a previous session
codex exec resume --last "Now fix the race conditions you found"
```

Key flags:
| Flag | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `--full-auto` | Auto-approve edits |
| `--json` | JSONL streaming output |
| `--sandbox <mode>` | `read-only` (default), `workspace-write`, `danger-full-access` |
| `-o <path>` | Write final message to file |
| `--ephemeral` | Don't persist session files |
| `--skip-git-repo-check` | Run outside a git repo |

---

**For overnight runs**, you'd typically combine with `nohup` or `tmux`:

```bash
# In a tmux session or with nohup
nohup agent -p --force "Refactor the entire API layer to use the new schema" > cursor-output.log 2>&1 &
nohup codex exec --full-auto "Add unit tests for all uncovered functions" > codex-output.log 2>&1 &
```

Sources:
- [Cursor CLI Headless Docs](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/headless)
- [Cursor CLI Agent Modes (Jan 2026)](https://cursor.com/changelog/cli-jan-16-2026)
- [Codex Non-Interactive Mode](https://developers.openai.com/codex/noninteractive/)
- [Codex CLI Reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/reference/)
- [Codex GitHub Repo](https://github.com/openai/codex)
